today_news.title: OpenAI's new training dataset teaches AI models which instructions to trust
today_news.summary: OpenAI has released IH-Challenge, a training dataset designed to teach AI models to reliably prioritize trusted instructions over untrusted ones. Early results show significant improvements in both security and prompt injection defense.

today_news.title: Google unifies text, image, video, and audio in a single vector space with Gemini Embedding 2
today_news.summary: Google's first native multimodal embedding model brings text, images, video, audio, and documents into one vector space, cutting out the need for separate models in AI pipelines.

today_news.title: The Download: Pokémon Go to train world models, and the US-China race to find aliens
today_news.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. today_news.title: Claude's Excel and PowerPoint add-ins now share context across apps
today_news.summary: Anthropic is updating its Claude add-ins for Excel and PowerPoint with shared context, reusable workflows, and broader cloud support.

today_news.title: Grammarly's AI writing tips claim inspiration from experts who never agreed to participate
today_news.summary: Grammarly is apparently using the names of journalists and authors without permission for an AI feature called "Expert Review."

today_news.title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
today_news.summary: Feng Qingyang had always hoped to launch his own company, but he never thought this would be how—or that the day would come this fast.  Feng, a 27-year-old software engineer based in Beijing, started tinkering with OpenClaw, a popular new open-source AI tool that can take over a device and auto...

today_news.title: Half of AI-written code that passes industry test would get rejected by real developers, new study finds
today_news.summary: About half of the AI code solutions that pass the popular SWE-bench benchmark would get rejected by actual project maintainers, according to a new study by the research organization METR.

today_tech.title: MultiwayPAM: Multiway Partitioning Around Medoids for LLM-as-a-Judge Score Analysis
today_tech.summary: Abstract: LLM-as-a-Judge is a flexible framework for text evaluation, which allows us to obtain scores for the quality of a given text from various perspectives by changing the prompt template. today_tech.title: Calibration-Reasoning Framework for Descriptive Speech Quality Assessment
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Explainable speech quality assessment requires moving beyond Mean Opinion Scores (MOS) to analyze underlying perceptual dimensions.
